skill.name: ast-grep
skill.description: Syntax-aware code search, linting, and rewrite guidance for ast-grep across supported programming languages.
skill.summary: Use ast-grep to find, lint, test, and rewrite code with AST-aware patterns.
skill.author: Dwsy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged eval text, command snippets, hardcoded URLs, weak crypto keywords, and entropy. Review found no executable code, no prompt injection, and no data exfiltration; the real risk is legitimate local CLI usage that can rewrite project files.

medium_findings.title: Project-Modifying CLI Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill documents ast-grep command examples, including rewrite commands. This is legitimate for the skill, but careless use can change many files in a project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly documented ast-grep rewrite workflows, not hidden execution. Risk remains because the intended tool can modify source files at scale.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Evaluation Alert Is a Search Pattern
low_findings.description: The eval text is an ast-grep pattern for finding unsafe JavaScript usage. It is not an eval call executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is inside a fenced bash example and invokes ast-grep with a pattern string. No executable eval implementation is present.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to ast-grep documentation, GitHub, catalog, playground, and MCP resources. No network request code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in a reference resources list. They are not used by scripts, download commands, or exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: Keyword, Reconnaissance, and Entropy Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are frontmatter text, ast-grep rule terminology, and a YAML rule keyword. Review found no cryptography, reconnaissance command, or encoded payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines contain descriptive Chinese text and rule names such as precedes and matches. No hash function, reconnaissance command, binary blob, or decoder was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bulk Rewrite Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill teaches ast-grep run with replacement or rule files. This can be useful, but it should be reviewed before applying changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is intentional and documented. It is dangerous only because bulk rewrites can alter many files if the rule is wrong.

content.user_title: Find and Rewrite Code with ast-grep
content.value_statement: Text search misses structure and creates noisy results. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use ast-grep patterns for precise code search, linting, and rewrites.
actual_capabilities: Explains ast-grep installation and command-line usage for supported environments.
Guides syntax-aware searches using patterns, node kinds, and metavariables.
Shows how to create YAML rules for linting and structured code checks.
Provides examples for testing ast-grep rules before repository-wide use.
Documents rewrite workflows for refactoring, API migration, and import updates.
Compares ast-grep with grep and ripgrep for code structure tasks.
limitations: Requires ast-grep to be installed before commands can run.
Does not include custom executable helper scripts or an automated safety wrapper.
Complex rules require knowledge of AST node kinds and language syntax.
Rewrite commands can change files and should be reviewed before applying broadly.

use_cases.title: Locate Code Patterns Precisely
use_cases.description: Find function calls, declarations, conditionals, or imports by code structure instead of plain text.

use_cases.title: Build Custom Lint Rules
use_cases.description: Create and test ast-grep YAML rules for project-specific code quality checks.

use_cases.title: Plan Safer Refactors
use_cases.description: Prototype rewrite patterns for API migrations, import changes, and repeated source transformations.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Simple Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ast-grep to find all console logging calls in this TypeScript project. Explain the command before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Lint Rule
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write an ast-grep YAML rule that detects async functions without await. Include a small test plan.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a Rule Match
prompt_templates.prompt: This ast-grep rule does not match my sample code. Inspect the pattern, identify likely AST issues, and propose a corrected rule.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Repository Rewrite
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an ast-grep rewrite workflow for migrating an old API to a new API. Include validation steps before applying changes.

output_examples.input: Find every logging call in a TypeScript package.
output_examples.output: A short ast-grep command, an explanation of the pattern, and guidance for reviewing matched files.

output_examples.input: Create a custom lint rule for a project convention.
output_examples.output: A rule design summary, a test example, and instructions for scanning the target directory.

output_examples.input: Migrate calls from one API name to another.
output_examples.output: A staged rewrite plan with pattern selection, dry-run review, test execution, and change verification.

best_practices: Test rules on small examples before scanning or rewriting a full repository.
Use version control and review diffs after every rewrite command.
Prefer precise patterns and AST node kinds over broad text-like matches.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad rewrite commands without inspecting expected matches first.
Do not assume a pattern works across languages without testing language-specific syntax.
Do not use ast-grep as a replacement for full security review or compiler checks.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It helps you use ast-grep for syntax-aware search, linting, rule testing, and code rewrites.

faq.question: Does it install ast-grep automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides installation commands and usage guidance, but installation is performed by the user or agent environment.

faq.question: Can it modify my repository?
faq.answer: Yes, ast-grep rewrite commands can modify files. Review matches and keep version control available before applying rewrites.

faq.question: Is the eval example dangerous?
faq.answer: No. It is a search pattern for finding eval usage in JavaScript, not code executed by the skill.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I choose ast-grep over ripgrep?
faq.answer: Use ast-grep when code structure matters, such as matching function calls, AST nodes, or rewrite targets.
